evidence: firstTtsAudioFired has only 4 references (grep): decl `private firstTtsAudioFired = new Set<string>();` (:224), `this.firstTtsAudioFired.clear();` inside closeOnce() (:386, session-close ONLY), and the read/add at handleTtsAudio (:868-869): `if (!this.firstTtsAudioFired.has(pkt.contextId)) { this.firstTtsAudioFired.add(pkt.contextId); this.watchdogs.clearVaqiIfContext(pkt.contextId); const userStoppedMs = this.turnUserStoppedAtMs.get(pkt.contextId); if (userStoppedMs !== undefined) { this.bus.push(... make.metric(pkt.contextId, 'vaqi.latency_ms', ...)); this.turnUserStoppedAtMs.delete(pkt.contextId); } }`. There is NO per-turn .delete() on eos.turn_complete, vad.speech_ended, llm.done, or tts.end — only the close() .clear(). Telephony contextId is pinned per CALL, not per turn: twilio.ts:294 `state.contextId = contextIdFn(start)` set once at the start event and reused for every media frame; defaultTwilioContextId returns `twilio-${callSid}` (:579), defaultTelnyxContextId returns `telnyx-${callControlId}` (:686). So on turn 2+ of a telephony call the Set already contains the contextId, the whole block is skipped: vaqi.latency_ms is NOT emitted and clearVaqiIfContext is NOT called. Consequence chain confirmed: handleVadSpeechEnded fires every turn and arms startVaqiMissedResponseTimer (:673); that timer is single-slot keyed on vaqiMissedResponseContextId and is only cleared by clearVaqiIfContext (skipped on turn 2+) or the next turn re-arming it — so if the user stays silent >vaqiMissedResponseMs (default 4000) after the assistant DID respond, a FALSE vaqi.missed_response fires (util.ts:122-130) and onVaqiMissedResponseFired deletes turnUserStoppedAtMs (:300). The existing test (voice-agent-session.test.ts:1155 'emits vaqi.latency_ms once per turn') only uses a single contextId 'turn-1' and asserts within-turn dedup; it never exercises a second turn on a stable contextId, so it does not catch this. The browser/websocket path is unaffected: index.ts:650-653 rotates contextId per turn (`nextContextId !== currentContextId` -> pushTurnChange), giving the Set a fresh key each turn.
real_fix: Make firstTtsAudioFired (and the paired vaqi/latency state) per-turn rather than per-session. Cleanest: delete the contextId from firstTtsAudioFired on the turn boundary so a re-used (telephony) contextId is treated as a fresh turn. The natural hook is handleVadSpeechEnded in voice-agent-session.ts (right where startVaqiMissedResponseTimer is armed at :673, fires every turn): add `this.firstTtsAudioFired.delete(pkt.contextId);`. Equivalently it can be cleared in handleTurnComplete (eos.turn_complete, :676). Then add a regression test that runs TWO turns on a single stable contextId (e.g. 'twilio-CA123') and asserts vaqi.latency_ms is emitted for BOTH turns and that no false vaqi.missed_response fires after assistant audio on turn 2. Severity is high not critical: the impact is metrics/observability corruption (lost per-turn VAQI latency + spurious missed_response) on every telephony call after turn 1 — it does not break call audio, turn-taking, or barge-in.
files: packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:224 (decl), :386 (close-only clear), :673 (per-turn arm hook to add a paired delete), :868-879 (gated emit/clear block); packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session-util.ts:117-137 (single-slot missed-response timer + clearVaqiIfContext); regression test in packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.test.ts:1155
